title: Command Generation Package Boundary
goal: Implement #431 by creating an internal, lift-out-friendly package boundary for generic command generation.
non_goals: Do not publish an external package.
Do not move workspace command definitions out of src/agentic_workspace/contracts.
Do not change command semantics or generated output locations.
Do not add first-contact product surfaces.

intent.outcome: Generic command-generation rendering lives behind a distinct internal package boundary, with root scripts acting as wrappers.
intent.constraints: Preserve existing generated outputs, proof routes, and workspace-owned command facts.
intent.latitude: Add package skeleton, move generic render helpers, and route root generation through the package.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the boundary requires publishing or moving runtime primitives.

execution.next_step: Archive the completed execplan, commit, push, close #431, then continue #432.
execution.proof: generated package checks, contract tooling tests, ruff, planning surfaces, and proof selector.

invariants: Root scripts remain available as maintainer entrypoints.
Agentic Workspace command facts stay workspace-owned.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #430 isolate generic command-generation machinery for future extraction

required_continuation.activation trigger: after #431 closeout, continue #432 schema ownership split

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: A concrete internal package boundary for later schema/check decoupling.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: #432 schema ownership, #433 generic loader/check decoupling, #434 consumer wrapper clarity, #435 lift-out criteria.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Dogfood package surfaces, commit/push milestone, close issue only after proof.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact internal module names and wrapper shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A change would alter command semantics or require an external package split.

active_milestone.scope: internal package skeleton and generator wrapper boundary

immediate_next_action: Archive completed plan and close #431 after commit/push.
invariants: Generated outputs remain unchanged after regeneration.
validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed packages/command-generation/ scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py pyproject.toml tests/test_contract_tooling.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/command-generation-package-boundary.plan.json --format json

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed packages/command-generation/ scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py pyproject.toml uv.lock tests/test_contract_tooling.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/command-generation-package-boundary.plan.json --format json
uv run ruff check src tests scripts packages/command-generation/src
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section workflow_obligations --format json
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "dogfooding closeout review" --format json
completion_criteria: A distinct internal package boundary exists for generic command generation.
The root generation script delegates to that package.
Existing generated package freshness checks still pass.
The boundary is not a new first-contact product surface.

execution_run.what happened: Created packages/command-generation as an internal workspace package, moved generic command package rendering and freshness comparison into agentic_command_generation.generator, kept scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py as a thin Agentic Workspace integration wrapper, and preserved existing generated outputs.
execution_run.scope touched: internal package boundary, root generator wrapper, workspace packaging metadata, planning residue
execution_run.changed surfaces: packages/command-generation, pyproject workspace members, uv.lock, root generation script, active execplan
execution_run.validations run: uv run agentic-workspace preflight --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed packages/command-generation/ scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py pyproject.toml uv.lock tests/test_contract_tooling.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/command-generation-package-boundary.plan.json --format json; uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --conformance --require-node; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests scripts packages/command-generation/src; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section workflow_obligations --format json; uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json; uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "dogfooding closeout review" --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json
execution_run.result for continuation: #431 is satisfied; #430 remains open and should continue with #432 schema ownership split.
execution_run.next step: Archive plan, commit, push, close #431, refresh external evidence, then promote #432.
